text.title: Modern BA Framework — Agentic Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: Selector and conformance-audit prompt patterns, recommended subagents, and LLM failure modes specific to framework selection.

example.note: Framework-selector prompt — requires explicit scoring, no default to BABOK.
example.code: Inputs: industry, regulation_set[], team_size, delivery_model, contract_clauses[], existing_standards[], stakeholder_count, audit_required (bool). Candidates: BABOK_v3, BA_Standard_2025, IREB_CPRE, PMI_PBA, BCS_Diploma, SAFe_BA, Agile_Extension_BABOK_v2, SWEBOK_v4_Reqs. Score each on: regulatory_fit, team_maturity_fit, vocab_overlap_with_existing, tooling_support, certification_availability, agile_friendliness. Pick ONE primary + up to 2 extensions. Produce vocabulary glossary (BA term -> chosen-framework term -> methodology slug). Emit ba-framework-decision.md. Halt for human sign-off.

example.note: Post-delivery audit prompt — checks framework conformance against produced artifacts.
example.code: Inputs: ba-framework-decision.md, list of produced artifacts in .aidocs/<feature>/. For each mandatory deliverable in chosen framework, check presence + quality. Emit framework-conformance.md with PASS/FAIL per artifact and remediation.

list.item: faion-brainstorm — divergence pass across all 8 candidate frameworks before converging; forces the agent off its default-BABOK prior
faion-feature-executor — after selection, executes routing planner using chosen framework vocabulary as context on every step
faion-sdd-executor-agent — wraps BA artifacts inside SDD spec.md / design.md / test-plan.md so the framework choice survives into delivery
faion-improver — periodic audit comparing produced artifacts vs chosen framework required deliverables; flags drift
General Task subagent — one per candidate framework, summarizes mandatory artifacts and audit hooks for the selector converge step

antipattern.description: LLM defaults to BABOK because it dominates training data.
antipattern.reason: Force structured candidate scoring on all 8 frameworks; without it, IREB, PMI-PBA, and BCS are never selected regardless of context fit.

antipattern.description: Agent confabulates clause numbers, KA boundaries, and IIBA version dates.
antipattern.reason: Pin a local fact-source (cached BABOK ToC + IREB CPRE syllabi) and require citations to it; reject any structural claim not present in the cached files.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-proceeds from selection to routing without a human checkpoint.
antipattern.reason: The human-in-the-loop checkpoint between selection and routing is the only place a real BA can correct the framework bias introduced by training data; auto-execution removes this control.

antipattern.description: Certification recommendations are IIBA-only, creating vendor bias.
antipattern.reason: Cross-reference IREB, PMI-PBA, BCS, and Scrum.org product-owner tracks before presenting to the user; single-vendor cert lists mislead procurement.

antipattern.description: Agent edits the framework README tables to "modernize" them on each run.
antipattern.reason: Confine edits to agent-integration.md and .aidocs/<feature>/; churning git history on the README tables destroys stable reference content.

list.item: Between selection and routing — human BA or PO corrects framework bias before any artifact is produced
At vocabulary glossary sign-off — confirms terms align with org language before downstream artifacts use them
Re-run trigger: regulation set change, contract change, primary delivery model change, or M&A integration
